components.name: D-glucose
components.source: main_text
components.evidence: were grown in MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose (MODG) as the carbon source (32). (Base MOD as cited contains 20 mM D-glucose; this study raises it to 30 mM.)

components.name: nitrogen gas (N2)
components.source: main_text
components.evidence: Strict anaerobic conditions (pO2 = 0%) were achieved by continuously sparging the medium with pure nitrogen gas (10 mL/h) pre-treated through a Hungate column (47).

media.base_medium: MOD medium (chemically defined medium for Bacillus cereus; Rosenfeld et al. 2005, derived from Glatz & Goepfert 1977), used here as MODG = MOD + 30 mM glucose

added: note: MOD not supplemented with extra metal ions -> mild iron limitation

sources_chased.what: base MOD recipe cited as ref 32 (Rosenfeld E, Duport C, Zigha A, Schmitt P. 2005. Can J Microbiol 51:149-158, doi:10.1139/w04-132)
sources_chased.how: recipe unavailable directly (paywalled cdnsciencepub 403), but quoted verbatim in open-access Toxins 2025 (PMC11860165) which cites Rosenfeld 2005 as [45]; also cross-checked against biorxiv preprint 2025.07.14.664712

media.notes: All salts/trace metals and glucose already in current_components are correct and match the primary recipe exactly. The only genuinely uncovered part (the '15 L-amino acids' mixture) is now fully enumerated with per-amino-acid concentrations from the verbatim Rosenfeld 2005 recipe. MOD contains NO added vitamins (none listed in the defined recipe) and no dedicated chemical reductant/sulfide; anaerobiosis is achieved purely by N2 sparging (pO2=0%). Glucose is 30 mM in this study (MODG) versus 20 mM in the base MOD reference.
